title: SC2 Agent Playbook
description: Per-matchup playbook consumed by the in-game bot. Generated offline by the strategist (Opus) from aiarena replays. SC2 type names follow python-sc2's UnitTypeId / UpgradeId enum spelling (PascalCase).

schema_version.description: Semver of the playbook schema this file targets.

matchup.description: Which matchup this playbook applies to. The bot loads the file matching self.enemy_race at game start. Terran specialist for v0; Protoss/Zerg matchups would extend the enum.

generator.description: What produced this playbook. 'manual' for hand-authored, 'opus-<version>' for strategist output, etc.

source_replays.description: Number of aiarena replays the strategist used to derive this playbook. 0 for manual.

notes.description: Free-form notes from the strategist or human author.

build_order.description: Ordered list of opening / mid-game steps. The bot executes these in sequence, gated by each step's trigger.

composition_targets.description: Target army composition by game phase. Each phase maps unit type names to ratios that should sum to ~1.0. The bot uses these to prioritize what to produce when production capacity is free.

macro_rules.description: Numeric rules that drive macro decisions outside of the scripted build order.

worker_cap.description: Hard cap on total workers (Probes / SCVs / Drones) across all bases.

gas_workers_per_geyser.description: Workers assigned to each gas structure (Assimilator / Refinery / Extractor). 3 = full saturation; lower starves gas to favor mineral-heavy comps.

supply_buffer_pct.description: Fraction of supply cap to keep as buffer before queuing the next supply structure.

expand_at_supply.description: Optional supply trigger for the natural expansion if it isn't already in build_order.

max_bases.description: Upper bound on simultaneously-active bases (Nexuses / Command Centers / Hatcheries).

reactions.description: Conditional adjustments triggered by scouted enemy patterns. Trigger and response are name-keyed strings; the BOT defines which names are valid (vocabulary lives in code, not in this schema). Unknown names should be ignored with a warning at load time.

trigger.description: When a build_step fires. Exactly one of supply / seconds / on must be present.

supply.description: Fire when our current supply count reaches this value.

seconds.description: Fire at this in-game time (game seconds, not real seconds).

on.description: Fire when a named in-game event occurs. Vocabulary defined by the bot (e.g. 'barracks_complete', 'factory_complete').

kind.description: produce = build a structure, train a unit, OR upgrade an existing structure (e.g. CommandCenter -> OrbitalCommand). research = upgrade. expand = take the next base (target should be the race's town hall). chrono = chrono-boost target (Protoss only).

target.description: python-sc2 UnitTypeId or UpgradeId name (PascalCase). Examples: SupplyDepot, Barracks, Refinery, Marine, Marauder, Reaper, Medivac, OrbitalCommand, CommandCenter, Stimpack, CombatShield.

count.description: How many instances of target to produce at this step. Ignored for research / chrono / expand.

chrono.description: Protoss-only shorthand: chrono-boost the producer of this step. Ignored for non-Protoss races.

only_if.description: Optional reaction trigger name. If set, this step only executes when that reaction has fired. Vocabulary defined by the bot.

skip_if.description: Inverse of only_if — skip this step if the named reaction has fired.

composition_map.description: Map of unit type name to target ratio (0..1). Ratios across the map should sum to ~1.0; the bot will renormalize if they don't.

trigger.description: Name of a scouted-pattern detector defined by the bot (e.g. 'ling_flood', 'mass_roach', 'mutas', 'nydus'). Snake_case.

response.description: Name of a response handler defined by the bot (e.g. 'wall_off_at_natural', 'tech_to_marauder', 'thor_or_cyclone'). Snake_case.

priority.description: Higher priority reactions win when triggers conflict.
